questions: Should admins manage scheduled posts only through the existing admin DM chat, or do you also want slash commands/buttons as a first-class UI?
For "retweets", do you mean native X reposts of an existing tweet URL/ID, or quote-tweets with new copy?
Must queued replies be able to target another queued item, or is replying only to an already-published tweet ID/URL enough for v1?
Is v1 allowed to use attachments from an existing Discord message as the asset source, or do you need arbitrary file uploads into the queue?
What exactly should "publish daily" mean: each queue item carries its own `publish_at`, there is one fixed daily publish slot, or the bot should post the next queued item once per day?
Which timezone should scheduling use for admin-entered publish times?

success_criteria.criterion: An admin can create, list, cancel, and retry scheduled social-post jobs from the supported admin surface, and each queue row persists `status`, `post_type`, `publish_at`, and normalized source/target IDs.
Due scheduled `tweet` and `reply` jobs publish through the existing sharing flow (`Sharer.finalize_sharing`) and successful jobs store the resulting tweet URL and tweet ID on the queue row.
Due scheduled `retweet` jobs use a native retweet/repost API branch and finish with either `published` or `failed` status plus the target tweet ID recorded on the queue row.
Queue claiming is DB-backed, so a single due row is posted at most once across poll cycles, restarts, or multiple bot instances.
If the bot restarts after jobs become due, overdue queued rows are picked up on the next scheduler pass instead of being lost.
Existing immediate `share_to_social` behavior still works for standalone posts and thread replies after the scheduling changes land.
A committed regression harness covers enqueue/list/cancel/retry, due-row claim idempotency, scheduled tweet/reply publishing, scheduled retweet publishing, and exits successfully.
Scheduled tweet/reply jobs reuse the current share pipeline instead of duplicating media download, caption, and announcement logic.
New scheduler logic stays isolated to a small queue module/cog plus DB helpers, with only minimal new branches in `tools.py`, `sharer.py`, and `social_poster.py`.
Manual staging smoke confirms one scheduled tweet, one scheduled reply, and one scheduled retweet publish correctly against a non-production X account.

assumptions: V1 queue management will live in the existing admin chat flow; no new dashboard or slash-command UI is required unless clarified otherwise.
V1 media-backed scheduled tweet/reply jobs will reference an existing Discord message for assets rather than introducing arbitrary file uploads into the queue.
Scheduling will use one-off absolute `publish_at` timestamps stored in UTC; recurring schedules are out of scope.
Reply jobs will target already-published tweet IDs/URLs, not other queued jobs, unless clarified otherwise.
"Retweet" means a native X repost of an existing tweet ID/URL, not a quote-tweet with added copy.

Scheduled tweet/reply jobs should keep the current opt-out checks, announcement-channel behavior, and first-share side effects by reusing `Sharer.finalize_sharing()` where that behavior is still correct.
`scheduled_social_posts` will be a new table separate from `shared_posts`, which remains the current deletion/first-share log rather than becoming the queue source of truth.
